post_generation.claude_raw_response:
Story 2 fits. Shipping rerouting around hormuz means longer supply chains and higher costs for everything moving through that corridor. That's more pressure on importers to lock in alternative energy sources.

[{"text": "cape of good hope traffic spiking is a real signal. every time the hormuz route gets sketchy, the case for not being dependent on it gets stronger. we have got to be ready with green alternatives when those conversations get serious.", "story_index": 2}]
